qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary file read via unauthenticated trace viewer HTTP endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a DNS rebinding attack on the Microsoft Playwright MCP Server due to missing Origin header validation (CVE-2025-9611, package @playwright/mcp). The finding describes an arbitrary file read vulnerability in the Playwright trace viewer HTTP server via the /trace/file endpoint. These are different vulnerabilities affecting different components: the advisory affects the MCP server (package @playwright/mcp), while the finding affects the trace viewer (package playwright-core). The root causes are also different: missing Origin header validation vs. missing path validation in a file-serving endpoint.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a DNS rebinding attack on the Microsoft Playwright MCP Server (CVE-2025-9611, package @playwright/mcp) caused by missing Origin header validation. The discovered qualifying vulnerability is an arbitrary file read in the Playwright trace viewer HTTP server via unvalidated path parameters in the /trace/file endpoint. These are distinct vulnerabilities in different packages with different root causes.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2025-9611 affects @playwright/mcp package, while the finding affects playwright-core package
Advisory describes DNS rebinding attack via missing Origin header validation
Finding describes path traversal file read via missing path validation in /trace/file endpoint
Different vulnerable code locations: advisory relates to MCP server connection handling, finding relates to trace viewer file serving
